description: Evaluate ESG considerations — ownership structures, board practices, remuneration, voting rights, material ESG risks and opportunities, and integration into investment analysis

ownership_structures.dispersed: No single shareholder with material control; principal-agent problem dominant
ownership_structures.concentrated: Controlling shareholder; principal-principal conflict with minorities

types_of_influential_shareholders.institutional: Active managers, index funds, pension funds
types_of_influential_shareholders.activist: Campaign for governance or strategic change

governance_evaluation.executive_remuneration: Alignment with long-term value; malus and clawback
governance_evaluation.shareholder_voting: One-share-one-vote vs dual class; proxy access

esg_risks_opportunities.materiality: ESG factors that affect financial performance by sector
esg_risks_opportunities.environmental_factors: Climate transition, physical risk, resource scarcity
esg_risks_opportunities.social_factors: Labour practices, product safety, community relations

integration_approaches.best_in_class: Tilt toward top ESG performers within sector
integration_approaches.thematic: Invest in specific themes (clean energy, water)
integration_approaches.impact: Target measurable social/environmental outcomes alongside return
integration_approaches.integration: Systematically incorporate ESG into traditional analysis

examples_of_integration.adjust_cost_of_capital: Higher WACC for elevated ESG risk
examples_of_integration.adjust_cash_flow_forecast: Penalise lower revenue or higher costs
examples_of_integration.valuation_multiple: Lower multiple for governance red flags

validation.valid_approach: integration_approach in [exclusion, best_in_class, thematic, impact, integration]

integrate_esg.description: Integrate ESG considerations into investment analysis

errors.message: integration_approach must be one of the supported approaches

anti_patterns.rule: Do not apply one-size-fits-all ESG scoring across sectors
anti_patterns.why: Materiality varies by sector — carbon is critical for utilities, irrelevant for asset managers; sector-adjusted frameworks (SASB, MSCI) are required

anti_patterns.rule: Do not equate exclusion strategies with impact
anti_patterns.why: Divestment transfers shares to another holder without changing company behaviour; engagement and voting produce real-world change

anti_patterns.rule: Do not treat ESG as purely qualitative
anti_patterns.why: Carbon emissions, board independence, controversy counts are quantifiable; integration requires explicit numerical adjustments to WACC, growth, or multiple

goals.description: Evaluate ESG considerations — ownership structures, board practices, remuneration, voting rights, material ESG risks and opportunities, and integration into investment analysis

success_metrics.measurement: All decisions have complete audit trails

constraints.description: All operations must be auditable and traceable

tradeoffs.reason: trading operations require precise execution and full audit trails

invariants: error messages never expose internal system details
